title: Income Tax Department Issues Crucial FAQs for AY 2026-27 ITR Filing
content:
# Income Tax Department Issues Crucial FAQs for AY 2026-27 ITR Filing

Ek important update hai for all taxpayers! The Income Tax Department has issued significant clarifications and guidance regarding Income Tax Return (ITR) filing for Assessment Year (AY) 2026-27 (Financial Year 2025-26). This comes as taxpayers gear up to file their returns under the Income Tax Act, 1961.

Here are the key takeaways from the recent FAQs and related announcements:

*   **Applicability of Old Act:** For AY 2026-27, the Income Tax Act, 1961, remains fully applicable. The new Income Tax Act, 2025, will govern tax years starting from FY 2026-27 onwards. So, all familiar deductions, exemptions, and tax regimes under the 1961 Act still apply for this filing season.
*   **ITR Forms Notified:** The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) had already notified all ITR forms (ITR-1 to ITR-7) for AY 2026-27 on March 30, 2026.
*   **Utilities Released:** The Income Tax Department has started enabling the filing process by releasing Excel utilities for various ITR forms. Notably, utilities for ITR-1 and ITR-4 were released on May 30, 2026, followed by ITR-2 on May 27, 2026, making online and offline filing options available.
*   **Important Due Dates:**
    *   For individual taxpayers in non-audit cases using ITR-1 and ITR-2, the due date is **July 31, 2026**.
    *   For non-audit cases using ITR-3 and ITR-4, the deadline is generally **August 31, 2026**.
    *   Belated returns for AY 2026-27 can be filed by **December 31, 2026**, subject to applicable late fees and interest under Section 234F and 234A.
*   **Updated Returns (ITR-U):** Taxpayers can file an updated return for AY 2026-27 under Section 139(8A) of the old Act within the prescribed timelines, even after the new Act comes into force.
*   **Recommendation for Early Filers:** While utilities are available, tax experts recommend waiting until mid-June 2026 to file. This allows time for employers to issue Form 16 (by June 15, 2026) and for banks/deductors to update all TDS details in Form 26AS, Annual Information Statement (AIS), and Taxpayer Information Summary (TIS). Filing with incomplete data can lead to mismatches and notices later.

This proactive release of FAQs and forms aims to ensure a smoother transition and clearer compliance for taxpayers.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.incometax.gov.in/)

*Published on: 1 June 2026*
